title: Heatwave in Europe: From Spain to Germany, an unprecedented 'red zone' sweeps across continent
subhead: France's neighboring countries are also experiencing record-breaking heat. While some in the south are more accustomed to such weather, Europe remains overall insufficiently adapted to climate change, despite being highly exposed to its effects.

'It's pure terror against civilians': In Ukraine, Kherson's residents live under constant threat of Russian drones
subhead: In this southern Ukrainian city, separated from enemy positions only by the Dnipro River, Ukrainians claim to have regained the upper hand against a weakened Russian artillery but lack the means to launch a counteroffensive.

title: France detects first ebola case in doctor returning from DRC
subhead: This is the first case ever diagnosed on French soil. Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu is monitoring the situation 'very closely,' according to his office, as quoted by Agence France-Presse.

title: 'A man in shorts is as competent as a man in a suit': Young people push back against office dress codes
subhead: Some young people push back against traditional dress codes that still prevail in many professional sectors. When temperatures rise, the façade cracks and debates become more heated.

title: Resistance fighter Marc Bloch inducted into Panthéon in moving moment of republican solidarity
subhead: On Tuesday, the tribute ceremony recounted the life of the historian, who was executed by the Germans on June 16, 1944. In a speech that served as an indictment against Vichy France and antisemitism, President Emmanuel Macron lamented the 'spirit of defeat' that, he said, permeates 'our public life.'

subhead: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

title: France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
subhead: A prolonged heatwave, rather than a brief spike, has arrived. Unusually high temperatures are set to persist across the country until at least the end of this week, exposing more than 90% of the French population to extreme and rare heat.

title: Ten years after Brexit, the City of London financial district is stronger than ever
subhead: When the UK left the EU following the referendum on June 23, 2016, the City of London financial district had to relinquish some of its activities that were focused on the European market. But it has since bounced back, recording strong growth in insurance and fintech.
